tier_justification: 1 work-unit, no irreversible/outward surface, above tier 0 because no existing check covers the goal state (new script, new privacy boundary)

evals.assert: the fixture test suite passes on the branch
evals.negative_control: corrupt scan_transcripts.sh (e.g. `exit 1` at top) and confirm the suite reports FAILED, not PASS

evals.assert: the sentinel string planted in a Bash command's arguments never appears in scan output over fixture-sentinel.jsonl
evals.negative_control: run `grep -q SENTINEL_sk_live_99xyz skills/workflow-audit/scripts/tests/fixtures/fixture-sentinel.jsonl` directly against the fixture (not the script output) to confirm the sentinel is genuinely present in the input, so the script's absence-of-leak is a real filter, not an empty fixture

evals.assert: a corrupt transcript line produces a distinct jq_parse_error diagnostic on stderr, not silently folded into zero-events
evals.negative_control: run the same command against a fixture with no corrupt line and confirm jq_parse_error does NOT appear (proves the detector fires only on genuine corruption, not unconditionally)

evals.assert: session recency/ordering under --last-sessions is derived from in-file message timestamps, not file mtime
evals.negative_control: an implementation using `ls -t` / file mtime for ordering would flip the result after the touch above; the test asserts the order is UNCHANGED post-touch, which a mtime-based implementation would fail
